transitions.note: New feature added to backlog. Scope: import training plans from external sources (Hevy, Strong, Fitbod, Jefit, spreadsheets, PDFs, photos, share extension, paste-to-parse). Research phase pending — awaiting prioritization.
Research complete — parser strategy, exercise mapping, AI paste support
PRD written. Multi-source parser, exercise mapping, AI paste support.
13 tasks. Parser protocol + exercise mapper + preview UI.
All tasks implemented. BUILD SUCCEEDED. Feature complete.
Reopened 2026-04-20 per UI-015: feature shipped infra + tests but no entry point exists. Partial ship flagged per open-items review § #12.

source_investigation_areas: CSV/JSON import — simplest format, used by Strong/Hevy export
PDF text extraction — reuse OCR from nutrition feature
Photo OCR — handwritten/printed whiteboard or printouts
Share extension — iOS native app-to-app sharing
AI conversation paste — ChatGPT/Claude/Gemini/Perplexity/custom GPTs full conversation or excerpt
AI prompt preservation — store the original prompt used to generate the plan, allow re-running via FitMe's AI engine
Exercise name mapping — ML or rule-based, 87-exercise target library
Progression rules — linear, wave, percentage-based, RPE-based
Program structure — day splits, phases, deload weeks
Format detection — auto-detect whether pasted text is markdown table, numbered list, prose, or code block

ai_agent_sources.description: Users frequently ask ChatGPT/Claude/other assistants for training plans. These plans come in varied formats — markdown tables, numbered lists, prose, code blocks. FitMe should parse all common output styles and optionally preserve the original prompt for regeneration.
examples: ChatGPT: '6-day PPL split for hypertrophy, 4 sets per exercise, 8-12 reps, RPE 7-8'
Claude: markdown table with exercises, sets, reps, notes per day
Gemini: prose description broken by day headers
Custom GPT: structured JSON-like output with progression notes
Coach bot (Trainerize, Perplexity): structured coach-style output with phase notes
parse_targets: Day/split names (Push, Pull, Legs, Upper, Lower, etc.)
Progression rules (add weight when RPE drops, etc.)
ai_agent_sources.regeneration_flow: If user saves the original prompt, FitMe can re-run it via its own AI engine (AIOrchestrator/FoundationModelService) to produce variations — e.g., 'same plan but with dumbbell substitutions' or 'adapted for my current readiness score'. This turns FitMe into a personalized plan generator that learns from external prompts.

success_criteria_draft.target: >80% of imports produce a usable training plan without manual fixes
kill_criteria: Parse time >30s for any supported format

competitive_research_targets: ChatGPT fitness GPTs — output format patterns
Notion fitness templates — markdown table parsing patterns
transitions_note_v2: 2026-04-11: Scope expanded to include AI agent prompt parsing. Users often generate plans via ChatGPT/Claude/other assistants — FitMe should parse these varied outputs and optionally preserve the original prompt for regeneration via its own AI engine.

known_gaps.summary: Parser / mapper / orchestrator stack + 23 tests shipped cleanly. Entry-point views (ImportSourcePickerView, ImportPreviewView) were built and tested but never wired into any tab, sheet, or navigation destination. ImportOrchestrator transitions state correctly but confirmImport() does NOT persist to TrainingProgramData. Annotated HISTORICAL 2026-04-17 (commit 0831f60) per audit UI-015.
